description:
Your Kubernetes operator team stores all environment-specific settings in a single INI file.  
    You have been asked to automate the generation of per-environment Deployment manifests and to write
    a small audit log of what was generated.

    1. Pre-existing material  
       • Configuration file:  
         /home/user/kube-operator/config/app.ini  

       2. What you must do  
       a. Parse every section inside /home/user/kube-operator/config/app.ini.  
       b. For each section create a separate Kubernetes Deployment manifest in  
          /home/user/kube-operator/manifests/ named  
          “<section>-deployment.yaml” (e.g. “staging-deployment.yaml”).  
       c. Write a single plain-text log file that captures exactly one line per section, located at  
          /home/user/kube-operator/output/generation.log.  

    3. Manifest format (exact indentation, two spaces per level)  
       Each generated *-deployment.yaml must be **word-for-word** identical to the following template,
       with the bracketed tokens replaced by values taken from its INI section:

       ---
       apiVersion: apps/v1
       kind: Deployment
       metadata:
         name: [app_name]-[section]
       spec:
         replicas: [replicas]
         selector:
           matchLabels:
             app: [app_name]
             env: [section]
         template:
           metadata:
             labels:
               app: [app_name]
               env: [section]
           spec:
             containers:
             - name: [app_name]
               image: [image]
               ports:
               - containerPort: [port]

       Example for a section “test” would become:
       (Make sure there is **no leading ‘---’ separator**; start with “apiVersion” on the first line.)

    4. Log file format (exactly two lines for the supplied INI):  
       Use the *fixed* timestamp 2023-01-01T00:00:00Z so automated tests have deterministic text.  
       Each line must be:

       2023-01-01T00:00:00Z ENV=<section> MANIFEST=/home/user/kube-operator/manifests/<section>-deployment.yaml REPLICAS=<replicas> PORT=<port>

       The final generation.log **must match byte-for-byte**:

       2023-01-01T00:00:00Z ENV=staging MANIFEST=/home/user/kube-operator/manifests/staging-deployment.yaml REPLICAS=2 PORT=8080  
       2023-01-01T00:00:00Z ENV=production MANIFEST=/home/user/kube-operator/manifests/production-deployment.yaml REPLICAS=5 PORT=80

    5. Directory constraints  
       • Create /home/user/kube-operator/manifests/ and /home/user/kube-operator/output/ if they do not already exist.  
       • All files must be world-readable; default umask(022) is fine.

    6. Verification checklist  
       ✔ Two YAML files exist with the exact filenames and contents described.  
       ✔ /home/user/kube-operator/output/generation.log exists with the exact two lines above (including newline at the end of the final line).  
       ✔ No additional files are created.

    Follow normal Linux shell practices; all actions should be possible without root privileges.
truth:
Initial file that is already present before the agent starts:
    Path: /home/user/kube-operator/config/app.ini
    Contents (including final newline):

    [staging]
    app_name=my-web
    image=example/my-web:v1.2.3
    replicas=2
    port=8080

    [production]
    app_name=my-web
    image=example/my-web:v1.2.3
    replicas=5
    port=80


    Expected files after the agent finishes:

    1. /home/user/kube-operator/manifests/staging-deployment.yaml
    Content:

    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    metadata:
      name: my-web-staging
    spec:
      replicas: 2
      selector:
        matchLabels:
          app: my-web
          env: staging
      template:
        metadata:
          labels:
            app: my-web
            env: staging
        spec:
          containers:
          - name: my-web
            image: example/my-web:v1.2.3
            ports:
            - containerPort: 8080


    2. /home/user/kube-operator/manifests/production-deployment.yaml
    Content:

    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    metadata:
      name: my-web-production
    spec:
      replicas: 5
      selector:
        matchLabels:
          app: my-web
          env: production
      template:
        metadata:
          labels:
            app: my-web
            env: production
        spec:
          containers:
          - name: my-web
            image: example/my-web:v1.2.3
            ports:
            - containerPort: 80


    3. /home/user/kube-operator/output/generation.log
    Content (final newline after second line):

    2023-01-01T00:00:00Z ENV=staging MANIFEST=/home/user/kube-operator/manifests/staging-deployment.yaml REPLICAS=2 PORT=8080
    2023-01-01T00:00:00Z ENV=production MANIFEST=/home/user/kube-operator/manifests/production-deployment.yaml REPLICAS=5 PORT=80
